text:
There was a woman who lived on top of a mountain. Every morning she would go on her walk to the waterfall at the bottom. One day she slipped on a rock and fell, smashing both her glasses and a book. She sat there weeping, feeling completely disoriented. How could she ever find the waterfall again?

Her neighbor, an elderly blind man who had lived all his life there, had noticed her grief. He approached cautiously and offered her his cane, guiding her safely down. As they approached the waterfall's edge, he whispered, "Miss me?"

She nodded, wiping her tears. "Yes, you're wise. But tell me this—I have no idea where I am. How should I find my way back?"

He explained the constellations and the trails, pointing out the familiar sights they'd passed.

The next morning, she set out again. By nightfall, with the stars her guide, she'd regained her bearings. She found the waterfall exactly as she remembered
